constraints: Sum of |S| over all test cases <= 5*10^6
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a non‑empty string S consisting only of characters '<' and '>'.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer – the length of the longest prefix of S that forms a correct sequence where every '<' is matched with a later '>' and no '>' appears without a preceding unmatched '<'. If no such prefix exists output 0.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For "<<>>" the whole string is balanced, length 4. "<><>" becomes balanced after each pair, final length 4. "<>>>" becomes balanced after the first two characters, then a stray '>' makes the prefix invalid, so longest valid prefix length is 2.

First string starts with '>' – no valid prefix. Second string "<><" returns to balance zero after the first two characters, so the longest valid prefix length is 2. Third string "<<>>><" is balanced up to the fourth character, after that an extra '>' breaks the sequence; longest valid prefix length is 4.
All eight characters form a correct sequence, therefore the answer is 8.

solution.approach: Treat '<' as an opening bracket and '>' as a closing bracket. Scan the string from left to right keeping a balance counter: increment for '<', decrement for '>'. If balance becomes negative, the current prefix is invalid and we stop. Whenever balance returns to zero, the prefix up to this position is a valid complete sequence, so we update the answer. The longest such prefix is the required length. This runs in O(|S|) per test case with O(1) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the length of the string. Overall O(total length of input).
